title: Path Sum

starterCode.python:
# Definition for a  binary tree node
# class TreeNode:
#	def __init__(self, x):
#		self.val = x
#		self.left = None
#		self.right = None

class Solution:
	# @param A : root node of tree
	# @param B : integer
	# @return an integer
	def hasPathSum(self, A, B):

hints: Use recursive approach to traverse from root to leaf.
At each node, subtract the node's value from the target sum.
When you reach a leaf node, check if the remaining sum equals the leaf's value.
If a node is null, return 0 (no path exists).
Check both left and right subtrees - return 1 if either path exists.
A leaf node is one that has no left and right children.
The path must be from root to leaf - not just any path in the tree.
